title: New Rules Aim to Stop AI-Generated Content Problems
subtitle: Major platforms are taking strict action against low-quality and fake AI-created material.

summary.en: Scientific preprint servers and search engines are changing their rules to fight poor-quality AI content. ArXiv will now ban researchers who submit papers containing obvious AI mistakes. Google has also updated its policies to punish websites that try to trick AI search tools. These moves aim to protect the quality of information online. Authors are now being held fully responsible for the accuracy of their work, regardless of how it was created.
summary.zh: 科學預印本伺服器與搜尋引擎正在改變規則，以對抗低品質的人工智慧內容。ArXiv 現在將封鎖提交包含明顯人工智慧錯誤論文的研究人員。Google 也更新了政策，以懲罰試圖欺騙人工智慧搜尋工具的網站。這些舉措旨在保護網路資訊的品質。作者現在必須對其作品的準確性負起完全責任，無論該內容是如何產生的。

paragraphs.en: The rapid growth of artificial intelligence (AI) has changed how people create content, from scientific research papers to online articles. However, this technology has also led to a rise in low-quality material, often called "AI slop." This includes fake information, incorrect references, and nonsensical diagrams. To address these issues, major organizations are now creating strict new rules to ensure that the information we read remains accurate and trustworthy.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智慧（AI）的快速發展改變了人們創作內容的方式，從科學研究論文到網路文章皆受影響。然而，這項技術也導致了低品質內容的增加，這些內容通常被稱為「人工智慧垃圾」。這類內容包含虛假資訊、錯誤的參考文獻以及毫無意義的圖表。為了處理這些問題，各大機構目前正制定嚴格的新規則，以確保我們閱讀的資訊保持準確且值得信賴。

paragraphs.en: One of the most important changes comes from ArXiv, a popular website where scientists share their research papers before they are officially published in journals. According to Ars Technica, ArXiv is now enforcing a new policy to stop the spread of AI-generated errors. Thomas Dietterich, a member of the ArXiv moderation team, explained that authors must take full responsibility for everything in their work, no matter how it was written. If a paper contains clear evidence that the author did not check the results of an AI tool, the consequences will be severe.
paragraphs.zh: 其中一項最重要的變革來自於 ArXiv，這是一個科學家在研究論文正式發表於期刊前，用來分享論文的熱門網站。根據科技新聞網站 Ars Technica 報導，ArXiv 目前正在執行一項新政策，以阻止人工智慧生成的錯誤資訊傳播。ArXiv 審核團隊成員迪特里希 (Thomas Dietterich) 解釋說，無論論文是如何撰寫的，作者都必須對其作品中的所有內容負起完全責任。如果論文中包含明顯證據顯示作者未檢查人工智慧工具產生的結果，後果將會非常嚴重。

paragraphs.en: As reported by The Verge, these consequences include a one-year ban from the platform. Furthermore, if a researcher is banned, any future work they submit to ArXiv must first be accepted by a reputable peer-reviewed venue. Peer review is a process where other experts in the same field check a paper for accuracy and quality before it is published. This ensures that only high-quality, verified research is shared with the public. Examples of "incontrovertible evidence"—proof that cannot be argued against—include fake references or meta-comments left by an AI, such as a note asking the user if they would like to change a summary.
paragraphs.zh: 根據科技媒體 The Verge 報導，這些後果包括禁止該作者使用該平台一年。此外，若研究人員遭到停權，他們未來提交給 ArXiv 的任何作品，都必須先被具備公信力的同儕審查機構所接受。同儕審查是一種由同一領域的其他專家在論文發表前，檢查其準確性與品質的程序。這能確保只有高品質且經過驗證的研究才會分享給大眾。所謂「無可辯駁的證據」——即無法反駁的證明——包括虛假參考文獻，或是人工智慧留下的元註解，例如詢問使用者是否要修改摘要的提示文字。

paragraphs.en: Dietterich noted that this process is careful. Before a penalty is given, a moderator must document the problem, and a section chair must confirm the decision. Authors also have the right to appeal if they believe a mistake was made. This is not the first time ArXiv has changed its rules. Last year, the platform limited the types of computer science review articles it would accept, noting that AI tools made it too easy to create long lists of information without adding any real value or new research insights.
paragraphs.zh: 迪特里希指出，這項程序相當謹慎。在做出懲處前，審核人員必須記錄問題，並由部門主席確認該決定。如果作者認為判決有誤，也有權提出申訴。這並非 ArXiv 首次修改規則。去年，該平台限制了所接受的電腦科學評論文章類型，並指出人工智慧工具讓撰寫冗長的資訊列表變得太過容易，卻無法提供任何實質價值或新的研究見解。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the world of science, the impact of AI is also changing how we use search engines. Google has recently updated its spam policy to address attempts to "manipulate" its AI models. According to The Verge, which cited Search Engine Land, Google now considers it spam to try to influence AI search results in a dishonest way. This includes "recommendation poisoning," where people try to force an AI to remember a specific website as an expert source.
paragraphs.zh: 在科學界之外，人工智慧的影響也正在改變我們使用搜尋引擎的方式。Google 最近更新了垃圾內容政策，以應對試圖「操縱」其人工智慧模型的行為。根據引述搜尋引擎產業媒體 Search Engine Land 的 The Verge 報導，Google 現在將以不誠實手段影響人工智慧搜尋結果的行為視為垃圾內容。這包括所謂的「推薦中毒」，即人們試圖強迫人工智慧將特定網站記憶為專家來源。

paragraphs.en: Some companies and individuals have been using new strategies to make their websites appear more often in AI-generated answers. This field is sometimes called "generative engine optimization" (GEO). For example, a journalist once used special tricks to get himself ranked by Google’s AI as the "best hot dog eating tech journalist." Under the new policy, these tactics are officially banned. Websites caught trying to trick AI search systems may face penalties, such as being ranked lower in search results or being removed from the search engine entirely.
paragraphs.zh: 一些公司與個人一直在使用新策略，讓他們的網站更頻繁地出現在人工智慧生成的答案中。這個領域有時被稱為「生成式引擎優化」(GEO)。例如，曾有一位記者使用特殊技巧，讓自己被 Google 的人工智慧評選為「最棒的熱狗大賽科技記者」。根據新政策，這些手段已被正式禁止。被發現試圖欺騙人工智慧搜尋系統的網站，可能會面臨懲罰，例如在搜尋結果中的排名降低，或是被完全從搜尋引擎中移除。

paragraphs.en: These changes reflect a growing concern about the reliability of information in the age of AI. As AI tools become more powerful, they can easily create content that looks professional but is actually full of errors or bias. By holding authors responsible for their work and punishing those who try to trick search engines, these organizations are trying to maintain a standard of quality. The message is clear: while AI can be a helpful tool, it cannot replace the need for human oversight, care, and honesty in communication. As technology continues to evolve, we can expect more platforms to adopt similar rules to protect the integrity of the information we rely on every day.
paragraphs.zh: 這些變革反映出人們對於人工智慧時代資訊可靠性的日益擔憂。隨著人工智慧工具變得更加強大，它們可以輕易創作出看起來專業，但實際上充滿錯誤或偏見的內容。透過要求作者對其作品負責，並懲罰那些試圖欺騙搜尋引擎的人，這些機構正試圖維持品質標準。訊息很明確：雖然人工智慧可以是實用的工具，但它無法取代人類在溝通時所需的監督、謹慎與誠實。隨著技術持續演進，我們可以預期會有更多平台採取類似規則，以保護我們每天所依賴的資訊完整性。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Low-quality, messy, or worthless content produced in large quantities.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此處並非指廚餘（常見義），而是指AI生成的低品質內容。文中：This includes fake information, incorrect references, and nonsensical diagrams. To address these issues, major organizations are now creating strict new rules to ensure that the information we read remains accurate and trustworthy.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A place or medium where an event or activity happens, in this case, a journal or publication platform.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 場所、發表平台（此處指學術期刊或發表管道）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 通常指活動場地，這裡指學術發表管道。文中：Furthermore, if a researcher is banned, any future work they submit to ArXiv must first be accepted by a reputable peer-reviewed venue.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The act of corrupting or damaging something, here referring to manipulating search results.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意為下毒，此處用於科技領域，指惡意操弄搜尋結果。文中：This includes "recommendation poisoning," where people try to force an AI to remember a specific website as an expert source.

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Send the arXiv AI-generated slop, get a yearlong vacation from submissions

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers full of AI slop

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: Google updates its spam rules to include attempts to ‘manipulate’ AI

quiz.question: According to the article, what is a specific requirement for a researcher who has been banned from ArXiv to submit work again in the future?

options.A: They must personally apologize to the moderation team.
options.B: They must have their work accepted by a reputable peer-reviewed venue first.
options.C: They must prove that they have stopped using all AI tools entirely.
options.D: They must pay a fine to the platform to regain their publishing rights.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，若研究者被 ArXiv 封禁，未來提交的任何作品必須先經過「reputable peer-reviewed venue」（具聲望的同儕審查機構）認可。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章強調的是對 AI 生成內容負責，而非全面禁用 AI；選項 D 文章中未提到罰款要求。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about ArXiv's attitude toward AI-generated review articles?

options.A: They believe AI review articles are the future of scientific publishing.
options.B: They encourage AI-generated reviews to save time for busy scientists.
options.C: They view AI-generated reviews as potentially lacking original research value.
options.D: They only accept AI-generated reviews if they are written by computer scientists.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，ArXiv 去年限制了電腦科學類審查文章的類型，理由是 AI 工具讓產出大量資訊變得太容易，卻未能提供「real value or new research insights」（實質價值或新的研究見解）。這暗示平台認為這類內容缺乏原創性。選項 A 與 B 與文章內容矛盾；選項 D 錯誤，文章並未限制僅限電腦科學家提交。

quiz.question: In the context of the article, what does the word 'manipulate' mean in the phrase 'attempts to manipulate its AI models'?

options.B: To influence results in a dishonest or unfair way.
options.C: To manually organize data into a database.
options.D: To improve the speed of a computer system.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到 Google 更新政策以應對試圖「manipulate」（操弄）其 AI 模型的行為，並隨後解釋這包括「recommendation poisoning」等欺騙手段。因此，這裡的 manipulate 指的是以不正當手段影響結果。選項 A、C、D 皆與文章中描述的「試圖欺騙搜尋系統」之負面意涵不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding AI and information quality?

options.A: AI tools are inherently dangerous and should be banned from all research platforms.
options.B: Search engines are the only platforms currently affected by AI-generated misinformation.
options.C: Human oversight and accountability are essential to maintain the integrity of information in the AI era.
options.D: The rise of 'AI slop' is a temporary problem that will naturally disappear as AI gets smarter.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章最後一段總結了核心觀點：儘管 AI 是強大的工具，但無法取代人類的監督、細心與誠實。文章透過 ArXiv 與 Google 的案例，強調建立規則與責任制的重要性。選項 A 太過極端，文章並未主張全面禁用；選項 B 錯誤，因為文章同時討論了科學研究與搜尋引擎；選項 D 錯誤，文章強調的是需要人為介入來解決問題，而非其會自動消失。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
